nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
### рҹҺЁ Image Generation (OpenAI)
Sends a POST request to the OpenAI `/v1/images/generations` endpoint.

- Uses `gpt-image-1` model
- Generates an image from a given prompt
- Returns a base64-encoded image (`b64_json`)

рҹ“Ң Output: `data[0].b64_json`

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
### рҹ§ҫ Convert base64 to File
Converts the `b64_json` field into a binary PNG file to use in the next step.

рҹ“Ө Output: Binary image under the `data` field

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content:
### вңҸпёҸ Image Editing (OpenAI)
Sends the binary image to OpenAIвҖҷs `/v1/images/edits` endpoint with a descriptive prompt.

- Model: `gpt-image-1`
- Format: `multipart/form-data`
- Requires a real file (not base64)
- Supports optional `mask` input

рҹ“Ҙ Input: Binary image from `data`
рҹ“Ө Output: Edited image in `b64_json`

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.content:
### рҹ§ҫ Final Conversion (base64 вҶ’ File)
Converts the edited image (`b64_json`) into a downloadable or previewable PNG file.

рҹ“Ө Output: Final binary image

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note5

parameters.content:
## рҹ§  Image AI Workflow Overview

This workflow uses OpenAI's image generation and editing APIs with the `gpt-image-1` model.

### рҹ”‘ Requirements:
- You **must use your own OpenAI API key** from {{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}
- Create a credential in n8n called `OpenAi AIFB account` (or use your own name)

---

### рҹ’° Cost Warning:
- This model is **powerful but expensive**.
- Each image costs **$0.020 to $0.190** depending on resolution and type.
- Always monitor your usage via the [OpenAI dashboard]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

---

### рҹ”Қ Why use `gpt-image-1`?
- Unmatched **semantic control**: you can edit specific parts of images with detailed prompts.
- Supports **multiple input images**, coherent edits, and future multi-modal tasks.
- Editing works with or without a transparency `mask`.

---

### рҹ”§ Suggested Nodes to Expand Workflow:
- **Webhook** (trigger via your frontend or app)
- **Telegram / Slack** (prompt image generation from chat)
- **Set node** (inject dynamic prompts or user context)
- **IF / Switch** (change behavior depending on prompt type)
- **Merge** (combine multiple image generations)
- **HTTP Request** (send generated images to external APIs or CMS)

---

### рҹ’Ў Example Use Cases:
- Marketing teams: generate product visuals on demand
- Designers: edit and re-style illustrations without Photoshop
- E-commerce: dynamic generation of themed mockups
- Content creators: create blog and social thumbnails in bulk

---

> вҡ пёҸ Don't forget to add rate limiting or batch controls if generating large volumes!

nodes.name: Convert json binary to File final

parameters.name: prompt
parameters.value: A cute red panda like dark super hero

parameters.content:
### рҹ§Ә Manual Trigger
Starts the workflow manually. Ideal for testing and debugging purposes.

### рҹҺЁ Image Generation (OpenAI)
Sends a POST request to the OpenAI `/v1/images/generations` endpoint.

- Uses `gpt-image-1` model
- Generates an image from a given prompt
- Returns a base64-encoded image (`b64_json`)

рҹ“Ң Output: `data[0].b64_json`

### рҹ§ҫ Convert base64 to File
Converts the `b64_json` field into a binary PNG file to use in the next step.

рҹ“Ө Output: Binary image under the `data` field

### вңҸпёҸ Image Editing (OpenAI)
Sends the binary image to OpenAIвҖҷs `/v1/images/edits` endpoint with a descriptive prompt.

- Model: `gpt-image-1`
- Format: `multipart/form-data`
- Requires a real file (not base64)
- Supports optional `mask` input

рҹ“Ҙ Input: Binary image from `data`
рҹ“Ө Output: Edited image in `b64_json`

### рҹ§ҫ Final Conversion (base64 вҶ’ File)
Converts the edited image (`b64_json`) into a downloadable or previewable PNG file.

рҹ“Ө Output: Final binary image

## рҹ§  Image AI Workflow Overview

This workflow uses OpenAI's image generation and editing APIs with the `gpt-image-1` model.

### рҹ”‘ Requirements:
- You **must use your own OpenAI API key** from {{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}
- Create a credential in n8n called `OpenAi AIFB account` (or use your own name)

---

### рҹ’° Cost Warning:
- This model is **powerful but expensive**.
- Each image costs **$0.020 to $0.190** depending on resolution and type.
- Always monitor your usage via the [OpenAI dashboard]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

---

### рҹ”Қ Why use `gpt-image-1`?
- Unmatched **semantic control**: you can edit specific parts of images with detailed prompts.
- Supports **multiple input images**, coherent edits, and future multi-modal tasks.
- Editing works with or without a transparency `mask`.

---

### рҹ”§ Suggested Nodes to Expand Workflow:
- **Webhook** (trigger via your frontend or app)
- **Telegram / Slack** (prompt image generation from chat)
- **Set node** (inject dynamic prompts or user context)
- **IF / Switch** (change behavior depending on prompt type)
- **Merge** (combine multiple image generations)
- **HTTP Request** (send generated images to external APIs or CMS)

---

### рҹ’Ў Example Use Cases:
- Marketing teams: generate product visuals on demand
- Designers: edit and re-style illustrations without Photoshop
- E-commerce: dynamic generation of themed mockups
- Content creators: create blog and social thumbnails in bulk

---

> вҡ пёҸ Don't forget to add rate limiting or batch controls if generating large volumes!

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking вҖҳTest workflowвҖҷ".
Open the "Edit Image (OpenAI)" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create image call" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Convert json binary to File" node (n8n-nodes-base.convertToFile) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Convert json binary to File final" node (n8n-nodes-base.convertToFile) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
